documents.title: Globex Corporation Q3 2024 Earnings Call
documents.content:
Operator: Good afternoon and welcome to Globex Corporation's third quarter fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All participants are in listen-only mode. I will now turn the call over to Rachel Torres, Vice President of Investor Relations.

Rachel Torres -- VP of IR: Thank you, operator. Good afternoon, everyone. With me today are Sandra Walsh, our CEO, and James Rivera, our CFO. Today's remarks contain forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties. We will discuss non-GAAP financial measures; reconciliations to GAAP are available in our press release.

Sandra Walsh -- CEO: Thanks, Rachel, and good afternoon. Q3 was an outstanding quarter for Globex. We delivered revenue of $890 million, up 22% year over year, exceeding the high end of our guidance. This was driven by accelerating demand for our cloud data platform and strong adoption of our new AI Analytics module.

Sandra Walsh -- CEO: Let me highlight the key metrics. Cloud ARR reached $3.2 billion, up 26% year over year. Subscription revenue was $730 million, representing 82% of total revenue, up from 76% a year ago. We added 85 new enterprise customers with annual contract values exceeding $500,000. Our net dollar retention rate was 118%, stable versus last quarter. Total customer count is now over 4,200.

Sandra Walsh -- CEO: On the product front, we launched Globex AI Analytics in August. Early traction has been exceptional. 30% of new enterprise deals in September included the AI Analytics module. The module uses proprietary large language models fine-tuned on structured data to generate natural language insights from complex datasets. We're seeing average deal sizes increase 25% when AI Analytics is included.

Sandra Walsh -- CEO: Internationally, revenue was $374 million, or 42% of total, up from 38% a year ago. EMEA grew 28% driven by large financial services deployments. APAC grew 35%, led by Japan and Australia. We opened a new data center in Singapore to support regional demand.

Sandra Walsh -- CEO: On the government front, we achieved FedRAMP High authorization in July, which opens a significant opportunity. We've already signed three agencies and have a pipeline of over $200 million in government opportunities. Government revenue was $45 million in Q3, or about 5% of total.

James Rivera -- CFO: Thank you, Sandra. I'll cover the financials. Non-GAAP operating margin was 18%, up 300 basis points year over year. This reflects operating leverage in our cloud infrastructure and disciplined hiring. Non-GAAP gross margin was 72%, up 200 basis points year over year, driven by improved cloud unit economics and the mix shift to subscription.

James Rivera -- CFO: Free cash flow was $210 million in Q3, representing a 24% free cash flow margin. We ended the quarter with $2.8 billion in cash and short-term investments. CapEx was $95 million, primarily for data center expansion including the new Singapore facility. We repurchased $75 million of stock in Q3 under our $500 million buyback program authorized in January.

James Rivera -- CFO: DSO was 38 days, improved from 42 days a year ago, reflecting better collections and a higher mix of annual prepaid contracts. RPO was $4.1 billion, up 30% year over year, providing strong revenue visibility.

James Rivera -- CFO: On headcount, we ended Q3 with 5,800 employees, up 15% year over year. R&D was 25% of revenue, sales and marketing was 30%, and G&A was 7%.

James Rivera -- CFO: For guidance, we're raising our full-year revenue outlook to $3.45 billion to $3.50 billion, up from $3.35 billion to $3.42 billion previously. For Q4, we expect revenue of $920 million to $940 million. We're also raising full-year non-GAAP operating margin guidance to 17% to 18%, from 16% to 17% previously.

Operator: We will now begin the question-and-answer session.

Operator: The first question comes from David Kim with Bank of America.

David Kim -- Bank of America: Thanks. Sandra, the AI Analytics attach rate of 30% in new deals is impressive for a product that just launched. How do you see that trending? And is there cannibalization of your legacy analytics offering?

Sandra Walsh -- CEO: Great question. We believe the attach rate can reach 50% or higher over the next 12 months as we expand the feature set and customers see ROI. On cannibalization, it's minimal. AI Analytics is positioned as a premium add-on, not a replacement. Our legacy analytics tools still serve customers who need deterministic reporting. Think of AI Analytics as the insights layer on top.

David Kim -- Bank of America: And on the competitive front, Snowflake and Databricks are also pushing AI features. How do you differentiate?

Sandra Walsh -- CEO: Our differentiation is threefold. First, our models are fine-tuned specifically for structured enterprise data, not general-purpose. Second, we have a governance framework built in—data lineage, access controls, audit trails—which enterprises require. Third, we're platform-agnostic; we run on AWS, Azure, and GCP. Snowflake is single-cloud, and Databricks is open-source-centric. We believe the governed, multi-cloud approach wins in regulated industries.

Operator: The next question comes from Emily Zhang with JP Morgan.

Emily Zhang -- JP Morgan: Hi, thanks. James, on the margin expansion, 300 basis points is significant. Is this sustainable, or is there a near-term investment cycle coming for AI?

James Rivera -- CFO: Good question. We are investing in AI—R&D at 25% of revenue reflects that. But we're also getting leverage from our cloud infrastructure as we scale. The margin expansion is structural, not just timing. That said, we'll continue investing in AI and go-to-market, so I'd model incremental improvement, not step-function gains. The raised guidance of 17% to 18% operating margin for the full year reflects our confidence.

Emily Zhang -- JP Morgan: And on the Singapore data center, what's the total investment there and when does it reach breakeven?

James Rivera -- CFO: Total investment is approximately $120 million over two years, with about $60 million spent so far. We expect the facility to reach cash flow breakeven within 18 months of full operation, so roughly mid-2026. It supports our APAC growth strategy and is critical for data residency requirements in the region.

Operator: The next question comes from Michael Brown with Citigroup.

Michael Brown -- Citigroup: Thanks. Sandra, on the FedRAMP authorization, the $200 million pipeline sounds significant. What's the typical deal cycle for government, and when could this become material?

Sandra Walsh -- CEO: Government deal cycles are typically 9 to 18 months from initial engagement to close. Given our FedRAMP authorization in July and the pipeline we're building, we expect government to be 8% to 10% of revenue by end of fiscal 2025. The DoD and intelligence community are particularly interested in our AI Analytics capabilities for mission data.

Michael Brown -- Citigroup: And one on capital allocation. With $2.8 billion in cash, are larger acquisitions on the table?

James Rivera -- CFO: We're open to larger deals if the strategic fit is right. Our M&A focus areas are AI and ML capabilities, vertical-specific solutions, and international expansion. We've been disciplined historically—tuck-ins in the $50 million to $150 million range. But we have the balance sheet for something larger if the opportunity arises. In the meantime, the $500 million buyback program reflects our commitment to returning capital.

Operator: The last question comes from Sarah Kim with Citigroup.

Sarah Kim -- Citigroup: Thanks. Quick follow-up on net retention. 118% is solid but has been flat for three quarters. What's the path to reaccelerating expansion?

Sandra Walsh -- CEO: We view 118% as healthy and sustainable. The AI Analytics module is a natural expansion vector—it increases deal sizes by 25% on average. As it penetrates the installed base through renewals, we expect net retention to move into the low 120s over the next few quarters. We're also seeing cross-sell opportunities with our data governance module, which launched last year.

Rachel Torres -- VP of IR: Thank you all for joining us today. We'll be at the Morgan Stanley TMT conference next week and the UBS Global Technology conference in December. Have a good evening.

Operator: This concludes Globex Corporation's third quarter 2024 earnings call. You may now disconnect.
